source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the canonical balsa / composite DBF wing architecture: one continuous main spar (UD carbon I-beam as specified in the original sample), approximately 12 rib stations distributed along the 1524 mm span (typical DBF spacing 100-130 mm), skin panels covering the upper and lower surfaces between spar and trailing edge, a secondary trailing-edge spar, servo-mounting brackets for the control surfaces, control-surface horns, and a wing-to-fuselage attachment fitting at centerline. The spar itself remains monolithic per the original prompt; all other parts are added to make the wing a flyable structure that can carry the 4 g pull-up and wingtip-hold loads.

full_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic main wing spar for a radio-controlled design/build/fly competition aircraft. The aircraft is limited to a maximum wingspan of 5 ft (1524 mm); the scoring rule multiplies empty weight by wingspan (rated aircraft cost). The wing is a tapered rectangular planform with root chord 350 mm, tip chord 250 mm, span 1524 mm (5 ft), dihedral 3 deg. The main spar is a single-piece I-beam running from wingtip to wingtip through the centerline; cross section 30 mm wide flanges x 3 mm flange thickness, web height 25 mm x 2 mm web thickness, uniform over the full span. Material: carbon/epoxy unidirectional prepreg (E = 135 GPa longitudinal, 10 GPa transverse, density 1600 kg/m^3, longitudinal tensile/compressive strength 1500 MPa, transverse 50 MPa). Mission-level loads: maximum take-off weight (MTOW) = 6.5 kg (aircraft + 4.0 kg payload); design limit load factor n_z = 4.0 g (standard structural demonstration/wing tip test). Primary load cases: (R1) 4 g symmetric pull-up with full payload: distributed lift equal to MTOW x 4 g = 255 N applied as elliptical spanwise load giving a root bending moment M_root = 0.5 * 255 * 0.4 * 0.762 = 39 N*m (approx); max fiber stress in spar flanges <= 1500 MPa / 3.0 = 500 MPa. (R2) Wing-tip structural demonstration: aircraft is held at the wingtips and shall support 2.5 x MTOW = 16.25 kg hanging from the fuselage without structural failure or permanent deformation; equivalent tip-load spar bending check: max von Mises <= 500 MPa. (R3) Takeoff distance: ground roll must not exceed 100 ft (30.5 m) with MTOW (non-structural, included for completeness). (R4) Spar mass <= 180 g. (R5) First wing bending natural frequency >= 25 Hz (hard-mounted at centerline) to avoid coupling with propeller 1P at typical motor RPM 4000 (66 Hz) within a 2x margin. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static for R1, R2) + modal (R5). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling explicitly NOT required at this gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 spar, laminate schedule, and a report listing spar mass in g, max fiber stress per case in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The deliverable is reconfigured as a full wing assembly around the monolithic carbon spar. Parts: the UD-carbon main spar (kept monolithic per the original brief), approximately 12 balsa/composite ribs spaced along the 1524 mm span, upper and lower skin panels, a secondary trailing-edge spar, control-surface servo mounting brackets, control-surface horns, and a centerline wing-to-fuselage attachment fitting. The main spar is the single part that governs R1 and R2. The ribs, skins, and trailing-edge spar transmit distributed lift into the main spar. The fuselage attachment fitting governs the root boundary condition for both R1/R2 static cases and R5 modal. FEM verification runs on the assembled wing only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Rib-to-spar bond DCR, skin-to-rib bond DCR, and attachment-fitting bolt DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a main wing assembly for a radio-controlled design/build/fly competition aircraft, built around a monolithic carbon spar. Aircraft limited to wingspan 5 ft (1524 mm); scoring multiplies empty weight by wingspan.

Tapered rectangular planform: root chord 350 mm, tip chord 250 mm, span 1524 mm, dihedral 3 deg. Main spar is a single-piece I-beam wingtip to wingtip, flange 30 mm wide x 3 mm thick, web 25 mm high x 2 mm thick, uniform over the full span. Material: carbon/epoxy UD prepreg (E1 = 135 GPa, longitudinal strength 1500 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3).

MTOW = 6.5 kg (aircraft + 4.0 kg payload); design limit load factor 4.0 g. LC1: 4 g symmetric pull-up with distributed lift 255 N giving root bending moment 39 N*m; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa (1500 MPa / 3.0). LC2: wingtip structural demonstration supporting 2.5 x MTOW = 16.25 kg; max von Mises <= 500 MPa. Spar mass <= 180 g. First wing bending mode >= 25 Hz hard-mounted at centerline. Takeoff ground roll <= 100 ft (30.5 m).

prompt.title: Design/Build/Fly wing assembly with monolithic carbon spar (5 ft span)
prompt.brief: 5 ft span tapered wing built around a full-span UD-carbon I-beam spar + 12 ribs + upper/lower skins + trailing-edge spar + servo brackets + control horns + centerline attachment fitting. Carries 4 g pull-up at MTOW 6.5 kg and wingtip-hold 2.5x demonstration. First wing bending mode >= 25 Hz. Spar mass <= 180 g.

geometric_constraints.main_spar_taper: uniform cross section across full span

material.name: Carbon/epoxy unidirectional prepreg (main spar); balsa + glass-epoxy skins; aluminum or 3D-printed nylon brackets

load_cases.name: wingtip_hold_2.5x
load_cases.description: aircraft held at wingtips, fuselage hanging

boundary_conditions.mounting: centerline wing-to-fuselage attachment fitting cantilevered at fuselage for structural; centerline hard-mounted for modal
boundary_conditions.loads: elliptical spanwise lift applied via skins and ribs into main spar; wingtip-hold fixes both tips

parts.name: main_wing_spar
parts.role: primary bending member; continuous UD-carbon I-beam from wingtip to wingtip through centerline; carries root bending moment under LC1 and wingtip-hold under LC2; monolithic (single piece) per the original prompt

parts.name: wing_rib
parts.role: spanwise rib station that maintains the airfoil shape and transfers distributed lift from the skin into the main spar
parts.count_note: Approximately 12 ribs on the 1524 mm span (127 mm spacing) is typical for DBF-class balsa/composite wings; teams may use more.

parts.name: skin_panel
parts.role: upper or lower skin panel; carries shear and local pressure between ribs, conforms to the airfoil loft, and provides the external aerodynamic surface
parts.count_note: Minimum 2 (upper + lower); teams often split further for construction access.

geometric_constraints.coverage: leading edge to trailing-edge spar, root to tip

parts.material_hint: Balsa + fiberglass/epoxy or Monokote over balsa

parts.name: trailing_edge_spar
parts.role: secondary spar at approximately 70% chord; anchors control-surface hinges and closes the torque box with the main spar and skins

parts.name: servo_mount_bracket
parts.role: local bracket bonded to the rib inboard of each control surface that captures the servo body and routes pushrod load into the wing structure
parts.count_note: Minimum 2 (one aileron per side); flaps add two more.

parts.name: control_surface_horn
parts.role: horn attached to each control surface (aileron / flap) that converts servo pushrod force into surface hinge moment

parts.name: wing_to_fuselage_attachment_fitting
parts.role: centerline fitting that couples the main spar into the fuselage; provides the cantilever root boundary for LC1/LC2 and the hard-mount for LC3; transmits root bending moment and shear into the fuselage

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X = chord (LE to TE), Y = span, Z = vertical

pass_fail_criteria.description: Bond DCR at every rib-to-spar joint, skin-to-rib joint, and skin-to-trailing-edge-spar joint shall be <= 1.0 under LC1 (4 g pull-up). Adhesive shear capacity is the governing limit.
4x M5 attachment-fitting bolt DCR shall be <= 1.0 under LC1 root bending moment (39 N*m) plus LC2 tip-hold reaction.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: laminated shell for spar and skins; beam for trailing-edge spar; solid for brackets and attachment fitting
analysis_types: linear static (LC1, LC2) on assembled wing

interfaces.mediated_by: epoxy bond at each rib-spar intersection
interfaces.load_path: distributed lift from ribs into main spar shear web

interfaces.mediated_by: epoxy bond at each rib-skin interface
interfaces.load_path: aerodynamic pressure from skin into ribs

interfaces.mediated_by: epoxy bond at each rib trailing-edge notch
interfaces.load_path: shear and torque from skin into trailing-edge spar

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded horn base with M2 retaining screws
interfaces.load_path: control-surface hinge moment into trailing-edge spar

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M5 bolts through spar web with backing plate
interfaces.load_path: root bending moment (39 N*m at LC1) and tip-hold reaction (LC2) into fuselage

fem_check_policy.rationale: The 4 g pull-up and wingtip-hold cases couple the spar, ribs, skin, and root attachment; lift is distributed across the full assembly. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original monolithic-spar prompt defines only the primary bending member; a flyable DBF wing that can carry the 4 g pull-up and satisfy R5 modal is inherently an assembly of spar + ribs + skins + trailing-edge spar + servo hardware + root attachment. The spar itself remains monolithic per the original requirement.
notes.speculation_caveats: Rib count (12), skin thickness, trailing-edge-spar location, and servo hardware are team-selected; the spar cross-section and the 1524 mm span are anchored to the original sample.
